θ.name: theta
θ.meaning: Model parameters — the trainable weights of a neural net or the fitted coefficients of a regression.

Σ.name: sigma (sum)
Σ.meaning: Summation symbol. Sums the indexed expression over the range below it.

σ.name: sigma
σ.meaning: Standard deviation. In neural-net context: also the sigmoid activation σ(x) = 1/(1+e^-x).

μ.name: mu
μ.meaning: Mean. The expected value of a distribution.

λ.name: lambda
λ.meaning: Regularization strength. Higher λ penalizes parameter magnitude more in the loss.

α.name: alpha
α.meaning: Learning rate, or a mixing coefficient depending on context.

ε.name: epsilon
ε.meaning: A small positive constant — typically a numerical-stability term added to denominators.

∇.name: nabla
∇.meaning: Gradient operator. ∇L is the gradient of the loss with respect to the parameters.

𝔼.name: expectation
𝔼.meaning: Expected value of the bracketed expression under the indicated distribution.

ℒ.name: loss
ℒ.meaning: The objective being minimized during training.

𝒩.name: Normal distribution
𝒩.meaning: 𝒩(μ, σ²) is the Gaussian distribution with mean μ and variance σ².

p.name: probability
p.meaning: Predicted class probability — output of softmax/sigmoid layers.
